notes.author: Codex
notes.body: Remain parked behind Quality core 00000SZ2CF255HWFA0G9XVA006 and shared consistency Feature 00000SZ2HY4R5J9V6AM5XVA00A; resume only after both foundations are merged.

failed_actions.message: The workflow adds a separate agent-based Quality implementation while FileQualityService and /api/quality/checks retain the command-based implementation. It also stores quality_enabled and quality_timing in both quality/settings.json and node settings with only one-way synchronization; workflow reads node settings while /api/quality reads the quality file.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: Quality recovery is nonfunctional: retry-quality changes a failed Goal to qa, but automation claims only todo Goals and prepare_started_claim always starts with WorkflowTodo. The preserved candidate therefore cannot re-enter Quality evaluation through the advertised retry path.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The tests do not verify supervised test execution. parse_quality_provider_output marks provider work confirmed from nonempty self-reported tool strings, and the full-workflow fixture passes by printing such JSON without executing the configured target-app test command. This is a false-positive integration path for the new process and evidence contract.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

failed_actions.message: Quality is not preserved as one shared capability: workflow uses the new provider-backed evaluator, while the API Quality surface still accepts and directly executes an arbitrary command through the legacy check runner, including returning success for an empty command. These surfaces now implement different pass semantics.
failed_actions.rule: Keep Refine agent-first: delegate adaptable reasoning and execution to installed agents through shared context and tools instead of duplicating it in bespoke product logic or surface-specific controls. Add first-class functionality when it provides durable coordination, state, synchronization, supervision, evidence, recovery, accessibility, deterministic integrity, or a materially better shared human and agent surface; preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

verdict.message: Four concrete governance violations were found in Quality capability ownership, evidence integrity, recovery, and cross-surface consistency.

violations.message: The workflow adds a separate agent-based Quality implementation while FileQualityService and /api/quality/checks retain the command-based implementation. It also stores quality_enabled and quality_timing in both quality/settings.json and node settings with only one-way synchronization; workflow reads node settings while /api/quality reads the quality file.
violations.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

violations.message: Quality recovery is nonfunctional: retry-quality changes a failed Goal to qa, but automation claims only todo Goals and prepare_started_claim always starts with WorkflowTodo. The preserved candidate therefore cannot re-enter Quality evaluation through the advertised retry path.
violations.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

violations.message: The tests do not verify supervised test execution. parse_quality_provider_output marks provider work confirmed from nonempty self-reported tool strings, and the full-workflow fixture passes by printing such JSON without executing the configured target-app test command. This is a false-positive integration path for the new process and evidence contract.
violations.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

violations.message: Quality is not preserved as one shared capability: workflow uses the new provider-backed evaluator, while the API Quality surface still accepts and directly executes an arbitrary command through the legacy check runner, including returning success for an empty command. These surfaces now implement different pass semantics.
violations.rule: Keep Refine agent-first: delegate adaptable reasoning and execution to installed agents through shared context and tools instead of duplicating it in bespoke product logic or surface-specific controls. Add first-class functionality when it provides durable coordination, state, synchronization, supervision, evidence, recovery, accessibility, deterministic integrity, or a materially better shared human and agent surface; preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented Goal `00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007`; the worktree is reviewable.

Key changes:

- Added the [Quality agent prompt](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007-round-1/src/prompts/quality-evaluation.md:1) with Goal/Round intent, candidate evidence, requirements, instructions, and explicit tests.
- Integrated independent, timing-aware Quality verdicts into [workflow behavior](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007-round-1/src/workflow/behaviors/mod.rs:220).
- Passes now require structured evidence and concrete supervised-tool outcomes for every test.
- Disabled Quality records `skipped`, never a false pass.
- Candidate, provider, malformed-output, and tool failures persist recoverable evidence.
- Quality API settings now synchronize `quality_enabled` and `quality_timing` with workflow configuration.
- Updated Quality settings UI, intent documentation, fixtures, and end-to-end coverage.

Deterministic verification:

- `cargo test --no-fail-fast` — exit 0; 400 passed, 0 failed.
- Full daemon workflow test — exit 0; 1 passed.
- `cargo clippy --lib --tests -- -D warnings` — exit 0.
- Node web tests — exit 0; 16 passed.
- Smoke provider fixture check — exit 0.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — exit 0.
- `git diff --check` — exit 0.
rounds.prompt: Implement Quality as an agent evaluation for every Goal candidate, parallel in concept to post-implementation governance but with separate evidence and verdict. Give the Quality agent Goal and Round intent, candidate and repository evidence, business requirements, instructions, and the plain-language test list. The agent determines how to run each test through shared supervised tools and returns structured per-test pass or fail evidence. Honor quality_timing; no tests passes without provider work. Preserve failures for recovery and cover no-op, pass, fail, provider or tool failure, and evidence persistence.

failed_actions.message: QA retry creates a new claim that snapshots the current quality_timing instead of preserving the failed round's timing. Changing pre_merge to post_build before retry makes a successful retry advance directly from QA to review, skipping the required build.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Quality records candidate_commit but never verifies that the worktree HEAD and contents still match it before or after provider and command execution. A provider or command can modify the worktree and exit successfully, producing passing evidence for code different from the committed candidate; the tests verify process execution but not commit correlation.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented and committed Goal `00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007`; the worktree is clean and reviewable at `a40ab0e`.

Key changes:

- Composed shared Quality core `c1e9a9a` and preserved candidate `58d5b76` in branch ancestry.
- Consolidated Quality around one settings file, service, prompt, operation path, and evidence contract.
- Removed the duplicate Quality prompt and legacy command-override API semantics.
- Added supervised per-test execution with observed process ID, exit status, stdout, and stderr correlation in [service.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007-round-2/src/tools/host/quality/service.rs:1437).
- Added genuine capacity-governed QA retry that restores the preserved candidate and enters directly at QA in [workflow/mod.rs](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ2CF28DJN97511XVA007-round-2/src/workflow/mod.rs:1073).
- Unified API and explicit-root CLI retry paths through that workflow capability.
- Added parity, recovery, false-positive, provider/tool failure, cancellation, and evidence-persistence coverage.
- Kept the UI-only sibling isolated; no invented merge was performed.

Verification:

- `cargo test --lib` — 433 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo test --test full_workflow -- --ignored --nocapture` — 1 passed, 0 failed.
- QA preserved-candidate retry test — 1 passed.
- False-positive supervised-execution test — 1 passed.
- `node --test tests/web_*.test.js` — 31 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo clippy --lib --tests -- -D warnings` — exit 0.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — exit 0.
- `git diff --check` — exit 0.
- Final Git status — clean.

Commits:

- `6eaec18` — Compose shared Quality core
- `20ddbd6` — Preserve Round 1 Quality candidate
- `a40ab0e` — Remediate always-on Quality governance findings
rounds.prompt: Preserve candidate 58d5b76c2f11f6eaba01f32af7086458ac076465 and remediate all four governance findings after the shared plain-language Quality core from sibling Goal 00000SZ2CF255HWFA0G9XVA006 has converged on main; inspect that landed implementation first and compose it instead of duplicating or replacing it. There must be one authoritative Quality settings model, service, agent evaluator, operation/process path, and evidence contract used by workflow, API, UI, CLI, MCP, and desktop; remove split node-settings versus quality/settings ownership and remove the legacy arbitrary-command API semantics rather than maintaining parallel pass rules. Make retry-quality truly re-enter the preserved candidate at QA through the workflow claim/behavior engine (not WorkflowTodo), with correct ownership, capacity, operation state, logs, cancellation, provider/tool failure, and recovery. Treat supervised Process outcomes as the evidence source: provider JSON may reference tool executions but cannot prove them by nonempty strings; correlate every test result with observed supervised execution/output and reject missing or fabricated evidence. Empty configured tests are the only successful no-provider no-op; an empty command is not a Quality pass path. Add focused cross-surface parity, QA retry/re-entry, supervised execution correlation, false-positive fixture, provider/tool failure, and evidence persistence tests. Coordinate with sibling Goal 00000SZ2CF2CP3NVV3J1XVA008 for UI-only composition, preserve isolated worktrees and serial review, and do not force or invent merges.

Preserve candidate a40ab0eb28498a6cdfc68e6c4e1b675d269052bf and fix the two Round 2 governance violations while retaining one shared Quality service and supervised execution path. (1) QA retry must preserve the failed round/claim's original quality_timing and resume the exact remaining stage sequence; changing current settings between failure and retry must not let pre_merge work skip the required build or otherwise reinterpret the preserved candidate. Persist and test the retry timing/context as authoritative recovery evidence. (2) Correlate every Quality verdict with the committed candidate: before and after provider and supervised command execution, verify the isolated worktree HEAD equals candidate_commit and the index/worktree are clean. Any provider or command mutation, HEAD drift, or dirty state must fail Quality with explicit evidence and must not be accepted even when the process exits successfully. Add focused failure-path tests for timing changes and successful-but-mutating execution, plus full library/full-workflow, clippy, fmt, diff checks, and independent governance. Preserve the candidate worktree and do not reintroduce caller-supplied command semantics or competing settings ownership.
